title: Pneumothorax Detection from Chest X-ray (PWDR)
sub_domain: Pleural-line and lung-edge recovery from chest radiograph with pneumothorax categorical readout
source_data_quality_note: v2 physical_with_discrete_readout (PWDR) genesis Principle. Wraps L1-031 X-ray radiography (existing in v1 genesis) with deterministic threshold rules on visceral-pleural-line detection and Light-index volumetric estimation to assign categorical pneumothorax labels {none, small, moderate, large, tension}. Universal in emergency / trauma / critical care radiology — every chest X-ray is reviewed for pneumothorax. UTSW Emergency Department + Trauma Center high-volume use.

gate_substitutions.conservation_substitute: softmax simplex closure on pneumothorax-severity ordinal readout {none, small (<15%), moderate (15-30%), large (>30%), tension}

discrete_readout.threshold_function: Pneumothorax(CXR) = step function on visceral-pleural-line presence + apex-to-cupola distance. Light index (1985): collapse_pct = (1 - (avg_lung_radius_collapsed / avg_lung_radius_normal)^3) × 100. Categories: none if no visceral pleural line; small if collapse<15%; moderate if 15-30%; large if >30%; tension if mediastinal shift, depressed hemidiaphragm, or hemodynamic instability per Roberts 2014 ATLS guideline. Alternative metrics: Collins method (apex-to-cupola distance + interpleural distance at hilum + mid-thorax), Rhea formula.
discrete_readout.underlying_physical_state: lung-edge / visceral-pleural-line geometric reconstruction + per-hemithorax air-fraction map recovered from L1-031 X-ray radiograph (frontal +/- lateral views)
discrete_readout.threshold_continuity_proof: Light index is continuous on lung-radius ratio (cubic continuous transformation); category boundaries at collapse_pct={15, 30}% are measure-zero hyperplanes. Smoothed-grading globally Lipschitz with constant ~0.04 per percentage-point of pleural-line-detection error.

related_principles: L1-031 (X-ray radiography — radiograph attenuation analytical core, physics_core_principle)
L1-029 (CT — gold-standard sibling for ambiguous cases, informational)

E.description: Pneumothorax Detection from Chest X-ray (PWDR): wraps L1-031 X-ray radiography analytical core with established trauma / emergency-radiology grading rules. Stage 1 (analytical, from L1-031): from frontal (+/- lateral) chest radiograph, recover lung-edge geometric reconstruction (visceral pleural line, lateral chest wall border) and per-hemithorax air-attenuation distribution. Stage 2 (deterministic threshold): apply Light-index, Collins, or Rhea formulas to estimate percent collapse; classify into severity bins; flag tension features (mediastinal shift, depressed hemidiaphragm). Difficulty tier delta = 3. Mismatch parameters: rotation_artifact, expiratory_vs_inspiratory_state, supine_vs_upright_acquisition, skin_fold_pseudo_pneumothorax, bullous_disease_confounders, bilateral_severity_disagreement.
E.forward_model: Stage 1 (L1-031): chest radiograph -> {visceral_pleural_line(x,y), lung_attenuation(x,y), mediastinal_position}. Stage 2: Light_index = (1 - (R_collapsed/R_normal)^3) * 100; severity = step(Light_index at [0, 15, 30]); tension_flag = (mediastinal_shift_mm > 20) OR (depressed_hemidiaphragm).
E.world_state_x: Per-CXR categorical pneumothorax label (severity + lateralization); for tension flag, per-CXR boolean.
E.observation_y: Frontal chest radiograph (PA or AP, typically 2500x3000 pixel FFXR detector); optional lateral view.

G.dag: L.xray_source -> L.attenuation_projection -> L.pleural_line_detection -> L.light_index_estimation -> L.severity_threshold_classifier -> int.spatial
coupling_constraints: pleural_line_to_lung_radius: visceral pleural line position couples to per-hemithorax lung radius; both are estimated jointly from the same radiograph

W.regime: Existence inherited from L1-031. Uniqueness conditional on pneumothorax visibility; small pneumothoraces near lung apex can be missed in supine acquisitions (sensitivity drops 30-50% supine vs upright). Stability dominated by skin_fold_pseudo_pneumothorax and bullous_disease_confounders (the dominant false-positive sources in clinical practice). Joint Hadamard well-posedness for the pneumothorax-detection forward established by Light 1985 (foundational Light index), Collins 1995 (Collins method), Rhea 1982 (Rhea method), Roberts 2014 (ATLS guideline), Rajpurkar 2017 (CheXNet 14-pathology benchmark), Tang 2020 (ChestX-ray14).

C.solver_class: linear-operator + image-segmentation [pleural-line edge detection + lung-radius estimation] + categorical-readout [Light-index threshold] | end-to-end deep neural [CheXNet, CheXpert, GoogleHealth pneumothorax model] with explicit physics-informed pleural-line regularization
C.error_bound: Inherits L1-031 reconstruction bound. Misclassification probability bound: Pr(severity_hat != severity_true) <= C_thresh * |Light_index_error| / d(Light_index, tau_boundary). Pseudo-pneumothorax false-positive rate ~5-10% on supine portable films per literature.
C.complexity: O(H * W) per radiograph for stage 1; O(N_pleural_segments * N_radius_estimations) for stage 2; total stage-1-dominated

recast_attempted.recast_path: Original framing: 'Classify chest X-ray into pneumothorax+severity'. Option-3 recast: 'Recover visceral-pleural-line + lung-edge geometry via L1-031 X-ray radiography; apply Light-index / Collins / Rhea formulas to estimate percent collapse; threshold-classify into severity bins'. Feasible because pneumothorax has well-defined geometric forward model on chest radiograph and severity scales are deterministic.

joint_well_posedness_references: Light RW (1985) Pleural Diseases. Lea & Febiger, Philadelphia. (Foundational Light index for pneumothorax volume).

Collins CD, Lopez A, Mathie A, Wood V, Jackson JE, Roddie ME (1995) Quantification of pneumothorax size on chest radiographs using interpleural distances: regression analysis based on volume measurements from helical CT. AJR Am J Roentgenol 165(5):1127-1130.
Rhea JT, DeLuca SA, Greene RE (1982) Determining the size of pneumothorax in the upright patient. Radiology 144(4):733-736.
Roberts DJ et al. (2014) Clinical presentation of patients with tension pneumothorax: a systematic review. Ann Surg 260(6):1068-1078. (ATLS guideline reference).
Rajpurkar P et al. (2017) CheXNet: radiologist-level pneumonia detection on chest X-rays with deep learning. arXiv:1711.05225.
Tang YX et al. (2020) Automated abnormality classification of chest radiographs using deep convolutional neural networks. NPJ Digital Medicine 3:70.

distinctness_audit.rationale: L1-031 recovers continuous attenuation map; L1-523 wraps with pneumothorax detection rule. Different solution_space (1D pneumothorax severity vs 2D attenuation); adds L.pleural_line_detection, L.light_index_estimation, L.severity_threshold_classifier primitives.

v3_metadata.clinical_context: Universal use in emergency / trauma / critical care radiology. Every chest X-ray reviewed for pneumothorax (specifically post-line-placement, post-procedural, trauma, mechanical ventilation). Tension pneumothorax is a life-threatening emergency requiring immediate decompression. UTSW Emergency Department + Trauma Center high-volume use. FDA-cleared autonomous AI: Aidoc PNX, Lunit INSIGHT CXR, Annalise CXR, Qure.ai qXR — all PWDR-shaped systems. Deep learning systems (CheXNet, CheXpert) achieve radiologist-level detection on benchmark datasets.
